replication_study.hypothesis: Participants in the contingent-information condition will have a higher one-dimensional (1D) strategy score at test than participants in the full-information condition (mean acc_1d_contingent > mean acc_1d_full).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Prolific bee category-learning replication dataset (analysis-data.csv), v1
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single study wave; two between-subjects conditions: contingent-information vs full-information; outcome computed in the test phase.
data_plan.sample_size: 474 participants; 96 trials per participant (64 learning + 32 test).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Subject-level 1D test-phase score (trials nested within subjects).
data_plan.access_details: Data provided locally in data/original/24/input/replication_data/analysis-data.csv; for analysis, mount to /app/data/analysis-data.csv.

qualification.explanation: This dataset reproduces the same experimental paradigm (bee category-learning task with four binary features and two relevant dimensions) under closely matched conditions but with a new, larger Prolific sample. It thus provides an independent sample to test robustness of the focal claim while maintaining high fidelity to the original design.
qualification.similarity_to_original: - Same task structure: four binary features, two relevant dimensions, and feedback manipulation (contingent vs full-information). See data-dictionary.tsv (replication_data) for variables like condition, relevant_dimensions, and bee_group.*; and initial_details.txt/post_registration.json describing the original manipulation and outcomes. - Same focal outcome: 1D score at test comparing contingent vs full-information, as stated in initial_details.txt and reflected in analysis.Rmd computation of acc_1d. - Original study context: MTurk online experiments with the same feedback contrast (post_registration.json; PDF summary).
qualification.deviation_from_original: - Participant pool and size differ: Original Experiment 1 used MTurk with n=101 (post_registration.json), whereas this replication uses Prolific with n=474 (analysis-data.csv; data-dictionary.tsv). - Statistical reporting differs: Original emphasized Bayesian credible intervals for mean differences (initial_details.txt), whereas this replication pre-plans an independent-samples t-test (analysis.Rmd) with effect size; credible intervals can be added if desired. - Data format differs: Original raw JSON per subject vs preprocessed CSV (analysis-data.csv) with derived fields (correct, correct_1d_a/b, phase).

data_plan.notes: The response column in analysis-data.csv is NA but not needed because correctness variables (correct, correct_1d_a, correct_1d_b) are precomputed. Subjects have 96 trials each (64 learning + 32 test). Ensure /app/data/analysis-data.csv is available when running analysis.Rmd. Classification thresholds for 1D/2D blocks follow the original description in the code comments.

planned_method.steps: 1) Load /app/data/analysis-data.csv. 2) Confirm inclusion set (all unique subjects in the file). 3) Compute per-subject 1D test score: acc_1d = max(mean(correct_1d_a), mean(correct_1d_b)) on test-phase trials. 4) Summarize group means by condition (contingent vs full-information). 5) Conduct independent-samples t-test on acc_1d across conditions; compute Cohen's d and 95% CI. 6) Optionally visualize distributions (histograms) and learning-phase strategy proportions. 7) Save any derived outputs to /app/data.
planned_method.models: Independent-samples t-test comparing mean acc_1d across two conditions; report Cohen's d. Optionally, Bayesian estimation to mirror original credible intervals.
planned_method.outcome_variable: acc_1d: participant-level one-dimensional accuracy during test (0-1).
planned_method.control_variables: None beyond randomized condition assignment; outcome computed on test-phase trials only.
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.0); packages: dplyr, readr, tidyr, ggplot2, stringr; base stats for t-test.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in condition means for acc_1d (contingent minus full-information).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Independent two-sample t-test (equal variances), with 95% confidence interval; report Cohen's d.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Compute means with na.rm=TRUE at the trial level; exclude a subject from the primary test if fewer than 30/32 test trials are available (preference: all 32).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal outcome; no multiple testing correction.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided alpha=0.05 for the t-test; replication success primarily defined by direction (contingent > full) and a 95% CI excluding zero, consistent with the original claim.

files.analysis.Rmd: R Markdown analysis script modified to read /app/data/analysis-data.csv, compute test-phase 1D scores by condition, run an independent t-test, and produce summaries/plots. Saves derived CSV to /app/data/analysis-derived.csv.
files.analysis-data.csv: Preprocessed per-trial dataset for the Prolific replication (474 subjects x 96 trials). Includes condition, phase, correct, correct_1d_a, correct_1d_b, and feature codes.
files.data-dictionary.tsv: Variable descriptions for the Prolific replication dataset, including condition labels and feature/bee group coding.
files.data_exp1.csv: Trial-level dataset consistent with an Experiment 1 format (not required for the primary replication test but available for cross-checks).
files.analysis.Rproj: RStudio project settings (not required for execution).
files.fake/*.json: Illustrative fake subject JSON files; not used in analysis.

codebase.notes: Paths updated to comply with /app/data policy (inputs read from /app/data; outputs written to /app/data). Dependencies limited to base R and tidyverse packages; papaja and Stan are not required. Entry point: knit analysis.Rmd or source its code chunks in R.

analysis.instructions: - Ensure analysis-data.csv is available at /app/data/analysis-data.csv. - In R, install required packages if missing. - Knit analysis.Rmd (or run its code chunks) to compute test-phase 1D scores and run the independent-samples t-test. - Outputs (e.g., analysis-derived.csv) are saved to /app/data.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare mean acc_1d in contingent vs full-information, the mean difference, 95% CI, t-statistic, p-value, and Cohen's d. Assess direction (contingent > full) and whether the 95% CI excludes zero. Optionally compare magnitude to the original report (.83 vs .75; CI [.03, .14] from initial_details.txt).
